title: Glossary - Nox Protocol
description: Definitions of technical terms used in the Nox protocol

terms.definition: On-chain access control mechanism allowing to define who can see (Viewer) or use (Admin) a handle. Permissions can be persistent or transient.
ACL role with full control over a handle: can perform compute, grant permissions (allow), and add viewers.
Ethereum Layer 2 targeted by cToken for its rapid growth and performance. TVL of $2.9B with 10x growth between 2024 and 2025.
Cryptographic proof that a component runs in an authentic hardware TEE with the expected code (verified via MRENCLAVE/MRSIGNER).
cToken business model: iExec (1st B) provides technology to Builders/Protocols (2nd B) who serve their end-users (3rd B).
Confidential DeFi - Decentralized finance with transaction and balance privacy. Nox is iExec's cDeFi protocol.
Encrypted form of confidential data (balance, amount). Stored off-chain in the Handle Gateway database, never publicly exposed on the blockchain.
One of Nox's 5 pillars: ability to integrate with the existing DeFi ecosystem and third-party protocols.
Confidential computation operation on handles (add, sub, div, transfer, etc.) executed by a Runner in a TEE.
Name used for external product communication. Wrapped version of an ERC-20 with encrypted balances and amounts.
Basic protocol operations: add(), sub(), div(), PlaintextToEncrypted(), select(). First category of computation primitives.
Third category of primitives: custom functions developed by protocol users.
Protocol executed by KMS nodes during Phase 1 to collectively create a threshold key pair. The private key is fragmented (t-of-n).
Elliptic Curve Integrated Encryption Scheme - Encryption algorithm used by KMS based on elliptic curves.
Ethereum standard for typed signatures. Used to verify Gateway signatures with digest including handle, owner, app, and createdAt.
Ethereum standard for transient storage (tstore). Used for transient permissions that are erased at the end of the transaction.
Fungible token standard on Ethereum. cToken allows wrapping any ERC-20 into a confidential version.
Encryption technology enabling computations on encrypted data. Alternative to TEEs used by competitors like Zama, but less performant.
Security property ensured by ephemeral keys: even if a TEE is compromised, it can only decrypt requests assigned after the compromise.
Unique 32-byte identifier stored on-chain that points to an off-chain ciphertext. Structure: truncated hash (26 bytes) + Chain ID (4) + Type (1) + Version (1).
Secure entry point of the system operating in a TEE. Encrypts user data, stores ciphertexts, and serves as interface to the Handle↔Ciphertext database.
High Net Worth Individual - Wealthy individuals, one of cToken's targets among Capital Allocators.
First version of the iExec protocol based on order system, PoCo and event scheduler. Different from Nox which uses handles and systematic listening.
Dedicated service (Rust+Alloy) that monitors the blockchain to detect primitive events (Add, Sub, Transfer...) and sends them to the message queue.
Trust Domain Extensions - Intel's TEE technology used by Nox to create hardware-isolated secure enclaves.
One of Nox's 5 pillars: compatibility with existing blockchains, notably Ethereum and Arbitrum.
Distributed vault that manages encryption keys. No single node can decrypt: collaboration of t nodes (threshold) is required.
Centralized coordinator of the threshold protocol running in a TEE. Serves as entry point to KMS nodes but cannot decrypt (no key fragment).
Value extracted by validators/miners by reordering transactions. cToken privacy protects against MEV.
Markets in Crypto-Assets - European regulation on crypto-assets. Nox enables compliance via selective disclosure.
Cryptographic technology allowing multiple parties to jointly compute without revealing their inputs. Alternative to TEEs.
Official internal name and protocol of iExec's cDeFi. Reserved for development (GitHub, technical docs). For external: Confidential Token.
Conductor that dequeues the message queue, assigns computations to Runners and supervises their execution. Can reassign in case of failure.
ACL permissions stored via sstore (EVM storage) that survive the end of the transaction.
ACL permissions using tstore (EIP-1153), less expensive in gas, automatically erased at the end of the transaction.
Primitive (formerly trivialEncrypt) that creates a handle for a public plaintext value, useful for initializing counters to zero.
Proof of Contribution - Consensus mechanism of iExec Classic. Not used in Nox which adopts a different architecture.
Hash calculated via keccak256(operator, operands, teeComputeManager, msg.sender, block.timestamp, nonce) before being truncated to form the final handle.
Wrapped version of an ERC-20 with encrypted balances and amounts. First Must Have product of the Nox MVP.
Remote Attestation TLS - Protocol combining remote attestation and TLS used for all communications between Nox internal components.
On-chain smart contract where TEE components register with their attestations during Phase 0.
Executor that performs confidential computations on encrypted data in a secure TEE environment.
Real-world assets tokenized on blockchain (real estate, bonds, etc.). Key segment targeted by cToken requiring privacy.
Ability to reveal information only to authorized parties (auditors, regulators) via the ACL system, without public exposure.
Mechanism allowing to create a handle for someone else by paying the fees on their behalf. Ownership goes to the public key declared in the payload.
Secure execution environment with hardware isolation (Intel TDX). Guarantees privacy and integrity of sensitive operations.
Main on-chain smart contract for receiving and coordinating confidential computation requests.
Threshold cryptography where the private key is fragmented among n nodes. At least t nodes must collaborate for any operation.
Traditional Finance - Traditional finance (banks, funds). Major target of cToken which requires privacy to enter DeFi.
Total value locked in a DeFi protocol. Key metric to measure adoption. ETH = $69B, ARB = $2.9B.
Read-only ACL role: can request handle decryption but cannot perform compute or modify permissions.
Conversion operations between public ERC-20 tokens and their confidential versions. Reversible at any time.
Zero-Knowledge Proofs - Technology allowing to prove a statement without revealing the underlying information. Alternative to TEEs.
Second category of computation primitives: more complex operations like transfer, mint, burn for confidential tokens.
Rust library used by the Ingestor to interact with the Ethereum blockchain and detect events.
Nox competitor using MPC (Multi-Party Computation) on Solana for DeFi privacy.
One of Nox's 5 pillars: ability to enable audit and regulatory compliance (MiCA, GDPR, FATF) via selective disclosure.
Off-chain Handle↔Ciphertext database managed by the Handle Gateway. Stores ciphertexts confidentially.
DeFi protocols and platforms that integrate cToken to serve their end users. Examples: Psalion, Centrifuge.
Example of RWA Marketplace targeted by cToken. Platform for tokenizing real assets requiring privacy.
Unique identifier of the blockchain chain (4 bytes) included in a handle's structure (bytes 26-29) to guarantee cross-chain uniqueness.
Confidential stablecoins planned for future versions of Nox. Not included in the initial MVP.
Practice of copying investment strategies of other traders. DeFi transparency facilitates this practice, which privacy limits.
Decentralized Autonomous Organization - Decentralized autonomous organization. One of cToken's targets among Capital Allocators.
Component of the EIP-712 digest including name, version, chainId and contract address. Used to sign Gateway messages.
One of Nox's 5 pillars: ease of use with simple tools for developers and institutions.
Hardware-isolated environment in a TEE where secure components run (Handle Gateway, Runners, KMS Manager).
Main blockchain targeted by cToken for its depth, institutions and RWA ecosystem. TVL of $69B.
Financial Action Task Force - International financial action group. Nox enables compliance via programmable auditability.
Nox competitor using FHE (Fully Homomorphic Encryption) for DeFi privacy.
Practice where validators/miners anticipate and execute transactions before users to profit from them. Privacy protects against this.
One of cToken's 3 product messaging pillars: enables new business models impossible with total transparency.
Nox competitor in the cDeFi space, known for its partnership with Circle.
Cryptographic hash function used to calculate preHandle and EIP-712 digests in the Nox protocol.
Code integrity measurements in Intel SGX/TDX TEEs. MRENCLAVE = code hash, MRSIGNER = signature key hash. Verified during attestation.
Minimum Viable Product - Minimum viable product. For Nox, the MVP includes Private Token as the first Must Have product.
Storage or computations performed off the blockchain. Ciphertexts are stored off-chain in the Handle Gateway database.
Data or operations stored/executed directly on the blockchain. Handles are stored on-chain, ciphertexts off-chain.
Handle ownership determined by the associated public key. Can be transferred via the sponsoring mechanism.
Data submitted in a transaction containing necessary information (public key, handles, etc.). Used notably for sponsoring.
First workflow phase: registration and attestation of TEE components in the on-chain Registry.
Initialization phase: KMS nodes execute the DKG protocol to collectively create threshold keys.
Encryption phase: the Handle Gateway encrypts user data and creates corresponding handles.
Last workflow phase: publication of the confidential computation result on-chain.
Unencrypted data in clear. In Nox, data exists in plaintext only in the Runner's TEE enclave during execution.
Basic computation operations available in Core Primitives: addition, subtraction, division, conditional selection.
Confidential token operations in Advanced Functions: transfer, creation (mint) and destruction (burn) of tokens.
Example of Professional DeFi Capital Allocator targeted by cToken. On-chain portfolio management platform.
Message system between Ingestor and Orchestrator allowing to decouple event detection from their processing and absorb load peaks.
Nox competitor using zk-SNARK (Zero-Knowledge Proofs) for DeFi privacy.
General Data Protection Regulation - European regulation on personal data protection. Nox enables compliance via programmable auditability.
Programming language used to develop the Ingestor, combined with Alloy for blockchain interaction.
iExec Classic architecture based on events and a scheduler. Nox uses a different approach with systematic listening.
Software Development Kit - Client-side software development kit allowing developers to integrate cToken into their applications.
Fragments of the private key distributed among KMS nodes in the threshold protocol. No single node possesses the complete key.
Difference between expected price and actual execution price of a transaction. Privacy protects against slippage caused by front-running.
EVM opcode for persistent storage. Used to store persistent ACL permissions that survive the transaction.
Threshold notation: at least t nodes out of n must collaborate to perform an operation. Guarantees security even if some nodes are compromised.
EVM opcode for transient storage (EIP-1153). Used for transient ACL permissions less expensive in gas.
Nox competitor using FHE, considered the market reference in the cDeFi space.
